artifacts.content:
From: emily.johnson17@gmail.com
To: laura.johnson@gmail.com
Subject: Pollen + drill (again)

Hey Mom,

Just wanted to give you a heads up before you get another random call from school like last May.

The pollen on the Columbia River High School practice fields has started to kick up again. Today during after-school JROTC drill my eyes were already itchy by warm-ups, and by the time we were doing formation near the far goalpost I was sneezing nonstop. It wasn’t quite as bad as that May 2024 day when I had to leave early and MSgt. Ramirez walked me to the office and called you to come get me, but it felt like it was headed in that direction.

I took one of the 10 mg loratadine tablets I keep in my backpack as soon as I got inside, and it calmed down enough that I could stay for the uniform inspection. I’m thinking once April really hits I should just take loratadine every morning before school so I don’t end up missing drill or a shift at Riverside Brew & Bagel on the weekends. The last thing I want is to be all congested on bar shifts when it’s busy and Elena needs me on bar instead of hiding in the back blowing my nose.

Can we swing by the Walgreens on NE Fourth Plain Blvd sometime this week to restock? I’m almost out in my backpack and I’d like to stash a couple in my JROTC locker too.

Love you,
Emily

From: Emily
To: Maya
Date: 2025-02-22

Emily: I swear my body is allergic to my entire life schedule 😂

Emily: So you know how every April–June the tree and grass pollen tries to murder me on the Columbia River HS practice fields? That May 2024 JROTC drill where I had to bail early and MSgt. Ramirez called my mom from the office is burned into my soul.

Emily: Now work is joining in. We closed at Riverside Brew & Bagel tonight and Elena had us use that *new* lemon-scented sanitizer from Sysco again.

Emily: 10 min into wiping tables my hands and forearms started getting all red and itchy. Déjà vu from February 2025 when she sent me to the back room for like 30 minutes because my arms were covered in hives.

Emily: I rinsed off in the employee sink, popped one of the 10 mg loratadine tablets I keep in my locker (literal lifesaver), and it calmed down, but still.

Maya: Omg Em that’s not normal??

Emily: I know it’s “mild” compared to like real anaphylaxis, but it’s annoying. Between that, the pollen, and the maybe-walnut thing at Grandma Patricia’s last March, I feel like I should just live in a bubble.

Emily: Remember when we were at her house in east Vancouver for Sunday dinner and I ate that slice of walnut banana bread and 20 min later my lips got tingly and I had those itchy red spots on my neck? Mom gave me a 10 mg loratadine from her purse and watched me for like an hour so we didn’t end up at PeaceHealth Southwest.

Maya: You really need an allergist tbh.

Emily: Probably. Add it to the list with SATs, college, and surviving pre-calc 🙃

From: emily.johnson17@gmail.com
To: elena.riversidebrew@gmail.com
Subject: Quick note about cleaning stuff / schedule

Hi Elena,

Hope your weekend’s going okay and the morning rush wasn’t too wild.

I wanted to follow up about something from our February 2025 close. When we used the new lemon-scented sanitizer from Sysco, I ended up with a rash on my hands and arms and you sent me to the back room for about 30 minutes. I rinsed off in the employee sink and took one 10 mg loratadine I keep in my locker, and it went away pretty quickly, so I didn’t think it was a huge deal.

It’s happened in a milder way a couple more times since then when I’m on dish or wiping down tables for a long stretch, especially if my skin is already dry from the weather. Between that and my usual spring pollen issues (the Columbia River High School practice fields are basically a pollen cloud every April–June), I’m trying to be more careful so I don’t end up missing shifts right when we’re getting busier.

Would it be okay if I wear gloves when I’m using that specific sanitizer or the really strong scented soaps? I’m totally fine sticking to the unscented stuff whenever possible too. I just want to make sure I can keep doing my full barista duties without my arms turning blotchy.

Also, just FYI, I’m still good for my usual Friday evening and some Saturday morning shifts. I’m juggling JROTC and Civil Air Patrol on Tuesdays, but I’ll send you my updated school schedule once I get my SAT prep class sorted.

Thanks for understanding,
Emily Johnson

metadata.subject: Quick note about cleaning stuff / schedule

artifacts.content:
Title: Health / food / random body stuff
Date: 2025-03-10

Things to remember so I don’t keep forgetting and then freaking out:

1. Spring allergies
- Mild but annoying seasonal allergy to tree + grass pollen.
- Worst every April–June, especially on the Columbia River High School practice fields during JROTC drill and PT.
- May 2024 = disaster drill where I had to leave early, sneezing/watery eyes/nasal congestion, and MSgt. Ramirez called Mom from the office to pick me up.
- Plan: start taking 10 mg loratadine in the morning once the pollen count starts climbing so I don’t miss JROTC, Civil Air Patrol, or shifts at Riverside Brew & Bagel.

2. Cleaning products at work
- Riverside Brew & Bagel, lemon-scented sanitizer from Sysco.
- February 2025 closing shift: hives + itching on hands/forearms, Elena sent me to the back room for ~30 minutes.
- Rinsed arms in employee sink, took one 10 mg loratadine from locker, rash went away.
- Ask Elena about gloves/unscented soap.

3. Possible walnut sensitivity
- Early March 2024, Sunday dinner at Grandma Patricia Peters’ house in east Vancouver.
- One slice of walnut banana bread → ~20 minutes later: tingly lips + itchy red spots on neck.
- Mom gave me 10 mg loratadine from her purse and watched me for about an hour. No ER/PeaceHealth trip because it calmed down.
- For now: avoid walnuts, write “no walnuts” on potluck sign-up sheets.

Overall: no anaphylaxis, no EpiPen, but pattern = my body is drama. Mention all this if I ever actually see an allergist.

Event: Sunday dinner at Grandma Pat’s (no walnuts!)
Date: 2025-03-09
Time: 4:30 p.m. – 8:00 p.m.
Location: Grandma Patricia Peters’ house, east Vancouver

Notes:
- Bring salad + Sophie’s favorite rolls.
- Help Grandma with groceries up the stairs and maybe fix her Wi‑Fi again.
- Remember last year (early March 2024): ate one slice of her walnut banana bread and about 20 minutes later my lips got tingly and I got a few itchy red spots on my neck while we were cleaning up. Mom gave me a 10 mg loratadine from her purse and watched me for about an hour; it went away, so we didn’t go to PeaceHealth Southwest Medical Center.
- Not officially a “real” food allergy but better safe than sorry, so:
  • Remind Grandma gently: “no walnuts for me, please.”
  • If there’s dessert with nuts, just skip it.
- Pack backup loratadine in my backpack anyway (also useful if the trees near her place are super pollen-y like they get every April–June).
- Ask Grandma again about stories of Grandpa Daniel and his time in the Air Force for my JROTC project.
- Try to leave by 8:00 p.m. so I still have time to finish pre-calc homework and review Civil Air Patrol aerospace notes before bed.

metadata.title: Sunday dinner at Grandma Pat’s (no walnuts!)

artifacts.content:
From: emily.johnson17@gmail.com
To: maya.smith@gmail.com
Subject: Life update / trying to be a functioning human

Hey nerd,

Taking a break from pre-calc to brain-dump at you.

Schedule is insane but kind of fun: school, JROTC, Civil Air Patrol on Tuesdays at Pearson Field, and Riverside Brew & Bagel Fridays + some Saturdays. Elena just posted the new schedule and I somehow scored mostly morning shifts, which is good for homework but bad for sleeping in.

Health-wise, I’m trying to be more organized. You remember the May 2024 pollen meltdown on the Columbia River High School practice fields when I had to leave drill early and MSgt. Ramirez called my mom? I’m not repeating that. I started keeping 10 mg loratadine in my backpack and in my work locker, and I’m going to start taking it every morning once April hits so I don’t end up sniffling through uniform inspection or espresso rush.

The work rash thing is still a little weird. During a February 2025 close, that lemon-scented Sysco sanitizer gave me hives on my hands and arms again. Elena sent me to the back room for about 30 minutes, I rinsed off in the employee sink, took a loratadine, and it calmed down. I emailed her about maybe using gloves.

And then there’s the walnut question mark from Sunday dinner at Grandma Patricia’s last year (tingly lips, itchy neck spots, one slice of walnut banana bread, Mom hovering with loratadine and deciding we didn’t need PeaceHealth). So far I’m just writing “no walnuts” on potluck sheets and pretending I’m not falling apart at 17.

Anyway, how’s your chem lab report going? Want to do a joint study session after CAP on Tuesday?

– Emily

metadata.subject: Life update / trying to be a functioning human

artifacts.content:
Title: Spring game plan – school, CAP, work, allergies
Date: 2025-03-25

Big picture for April–June:

School
- Keep GPA ≥ 3.7. Honors English essay on aerospace ethics + chem lab reports.
- Start SAT prep (aim for at least 1300). Maybe Saturday mornings before Riverside shifts.

JROTC / CAP
- JROTC drill on Columbia River HS practice fields = peak pollen zone. Remember May 2024 fiasco when I had to leave early and MSgt. Ramirez called Mom because I couldn’t stop sneezing.
- Civil Air Patrol Tuesday nights at Pearson Field – bring aerospace notebook + uniform checklist.

Allergies / health
- Seasonal pollen allergy: start taking 10 mg loratadine every morning once April starts, not just “when I remember.” Keep backup tablets in backpack + CAP bag.
- Work skin stuff: February 2025 closing shift at Riverside Brew & Bagel → lemon-scented sanitizer from Sysco = hives on hands/forearms. Elena sent me to the back room ~30 minutes; rinsed in employee sink, took 10 mg loratadine from locker, rash gone.
  • Follow up with Elena about gloves/unscented soap.
- Food: early March 2024 at Grandma Patricia’s in east Vancouver, walnut banana bread episode (tingly lips, itchy neck spots ~20 min later, Mom gave me loratadine, watched me ~1 hour, no PeaceHealth trip).
  • Keep avoiding walnuts, write “no walnuts” on potluck sheets.

Other
- Run with Daisy in the park 2–3x/week (watch pollen counts).
- Put $50 from each paycheck into college/flight lesson savings.
- Remember backup glasses in backpack in case contacts freak out from pollen.

metadata.title: Spring game plan – school, CAP, work, allergies
